park_name: Daylesford Holiday Park by Tiny Away

reviews.review_text: Fantastic place to take the pups. I stayed at the bark royal. Very clean! Park spotless. Quiet too.Will definately be back. Reception very nice and friendly|10 out of 10

Lovely caravan park with cabins. Very pleasant and helpful staff. Would thoroughly recommend. The cabins were very cosy and very clean. Reasonably priced cabins. Will most certainly be coming back again.

This park is beautifully tree lined with huge old trees. I was at an unpowered site and the amenities and camp kitchen were spotless.
They have another building with four fireplaces, lounges and TV. There is an outdoor area with lots of tables. Great for group gatherings. Lake Daylesford is a ten minute walk away. Perfect.

We had a beautiful powered site easy to get in with our van reception lady Kim was very helpful and friendly amenities are clean and tidy will be back again when in lovely daylesford also pet friendly

We recently stayed at the powered site at Daylesford Holiday Park and had such a lovely experience. The park is in a beautiful location, surrounded by nature, yet still close to the town centre, making it very convenient for families.

The powered site was spacious, clean, and easy to access, The facilities were well-maintained, especially the bathrooms and kitchen areas, which we really appreciated during our stay.

Our kids had a great time enjoying the open space and playground, and the overall atmosphere was peaceful and family-friendly. It’s a perfect spot to relax and unwind.

The staff were friendly and helpful, always greeting us with a smile and happy to assist whenever needed.

One small thing to be aware of is that it can be a bit noisy at night at times, but it didn’t take away too much from our overall experience.

Overall, we had a wonderful stay and would definitely come back again. Highly recommend for families looking for a relaxing getaway in Daylesford!

Friendly, helpful staff and a lovely setting among trees, but overall disappointing. We had to change sites due to a steep slope (staff were accommodating). Amenities are dated or not very clean, with mould and poor ventilation. Expensive for what’s offered compared to similar parks, and the WiFi didn’t work.

We held our Annual family get together with 50 family members attending. The staff were incredibly welcoming and extremely helpful to each and every family member. The park itself is really peaceful with beautiful surroundings. Definitely recommend!

Stayed one night - booking made months ago. Staff super friendly. Experience just odd. Booked online (as we are encouraged to do). Learn that because made online the G’day affiliation not recognised nor discount!!!  And again because made online the booking “buried in system”. The site we were originally allocated when the booking was made had been reassigned. Were given two other options both unpleasant. Noticed some better sites were vacant (apparently due to a late cancellation and an early departure that staff weren’t aware of). Managed to relocate to better site - particularly given the wet weather and the mud etc we were experiencing on previous sites. Would consider the tariff expensive compared to other van parks. Location an easy drive in to town and Daylesford town worth the visit. But if online booking is not effective perhaps users need to be made aware as certainly somewhat frustrating - and thankfully we’re only there for one night. Again staff were friendly and trying to do their job
Great place, clean, great staff

Clean, friendly staff, closeish to a lovely part of the world, beautifully treed grounds.
Wouldn't hesitate to book again

What a great park & great staff as soon as you arrive you are welcomed by the most amazing manager Kim & all the staff are fabo also. We were there for Chillout & the turned did its best to make everyone feel inclusive as did the whole town. Thanks see ya next year.

The new owners and managers have really focused on changing the experience at the holiday park. The amenities blocks were much cleaner. The site appears to be more cared for and it’s still located in the most spectacular part of Daylesford. Surrounded by spectacular old trees!

Absolutely love staying here, and the changes being made will make it even more enjoyable.

I could not fault the service from this place even if I really tried to. Amazing park and even better staff. A few hiccups with me turning up a day early (oops) but they made some changes for me and I couldn’t be more thankful. So accomodating and welcoming!!! Love love love it! We will be back every year 💜 🌈
Kim at reception is AMAZING! Love her!

Stayed in the unpowered section. Not a lot of flat area, dirty walk to amenities in wet weather. Amenities ok, camp kitchen ok. Staff very lovely. Very over priced park for what you get for unpowered stay. Wouldn't bother again.

We had previously stayed at this park and it has gone downhill dramatically, maintaince of the grounds is non existent. There are 2 camp kitchens and the stove in neither works. The tv doesn’t work, the microwave is filthy. Speaking to some permanents we were told that the park is being left to run down. We tried to get into the other park in town and it was full, this park had 4 vans only ….. no wonder.
Had a lovely time at the Holiday Park, the cabins have all you need, fantastic service from the staff. Close to town

Kim provided outstanding customer service from start to finish. She was incredibly helpful, friendly, and patient, especially when it came to guiding me on how to find the location. Her clear directions and willingness to go the extra mile made everything so easy and stress-free. Truly a great example of excellent customer service—thank you, Kim!
Staff very friendly we got a good site there are some large trees

Staff and managers Kim & Stewart are so friendly and helpful...& the park is lovely and clean and well maintained..! We loved our stay there are will definately be back in future...Jen & Russell...Charlie Macey and Narla xxx

We had a fantastic stay at Daylesford Holiday Park on a powered site with our caravan and couldn’t have been happier with the experience. From the moment we arrived, the park felt welcoming, calm and very well cared for.

Our powered site was generous in size, level, and easy to access, which made setting up the caravan simple and stress free. The power and water connections were conveniently placed, and there was plenty of space around us without feeling cramped by neighbouring sites. The grounds are beautiful, with lots of greenery large trees and a peaceful atmosphere that really suits the Daylesford
district.
The shower water pressure was amazing and the camp kitchen had everything we needed to whip up a meal.(free electric bbq, oven, hot plates. For dishes dishwashing soap, scourers and tea towels provided)

The location is excellent. It’s close enough to explore easily Daylesford’s lakes, and drive 5 minutes to markets, cafes and spas, but far enough away to feel quiet and restful at the end of the day. The park itself feels safe, family friendly and relaxed, with a lovely balance of nature and comfort, and best of all pet friendly.

Staff were friendly, helpful and approachable, and it was clear they take pride in the park and genuinely care about guests having a good stay.

We thoroughly enjoyed our time here and would absolutely stay again. Daylesford Holiday Park is a great option for caravan travellers looking for a clean, comfortable, well located and peaceful place to stay. Highly recommended.
Booked a caravan site and due to possibly late booking had a powered tent site, quite a slope. But once set up all was great. Close to town and facilities good. Enjoyed our stay
Just out of town but close to all. Caravan sites were good and facilities clean.

The room was extremely tiny compared to the photos for the 3 people that we booked for.
Even tinier kitchen and not enough amenities.
Lovely Park, Excellent hosts. Quiet and relaxing.

Great that when I called to arrive earlier that day for our site, it was not a problem ( this was great ).
The park had some seriously old and dated on-site vans which are an eyesore.
Bathrooms were clean, just one shower from.Sat to Mon was needing attention and thought cleaner would have picked up on this.
Would I stay again, yes, its a great town and the off lead area next to van Park was a bonus.
Made a stop here, found park very clean and great staff service.
Clean and working. 👍

It is a pretty park but arrived late and had trouble trying to get access to the park. |We have a large van so sites were limited. Hard to find site as not clearly marked. Had to search for power and water also not clearly marked. |Had problems with the site above us running their sullage down hill and going under our van. Luckily we hadn't put any flooring down. Outside became a muddy mess.|Amenities pretty average too.|Park has great potential but needs to be more organised and maintained.
Nice quiet and fairly clean place. It was a nightmare getting close to what resembles flat was time consuming and we moved multiple times

I stayed in Cabin 13 Maple. Had issues from the start. Exhaust fan in the kitchen not working, no door dividing the bedroom to the kitchen, couldn't open the kitchen window due to being locked no key available. Front sliding door fly screen off. Contacted office they sent a handy man. Fixed front fly screen, I unbolted the lock for the kitchen window (they did not have a key). Exhaust fan in kithchen never fixed. Other than that I had a comfortable week, stayed 7 nights. Ps to whom it may concern alot of dust throughout the place top shelves in cupboards top of fridge oven full of crumbs house keeping 3☆ as rated. Thank you to staff that helped with my issues.
Do not expect them to honour Big 4 membership discount. Very dated amenities, very crowded uneven sites. Beautiful trees though.

Not the greatest! As working parents of 4, we decided to book and stay in ‘The Wattle’ whilst having some amazing surrounding nature, the services in the cabin left a lot to be desired. Arriving for check in at 3pm, we found there was no coffee, sugar, milk, soap or bedding on the bunk beds, it was required to make the bunk beds and go to the supermarket to obtain things in which we would have expected to be there as per the room details. We did attend the office at 430pm which stated closed at 5pm however was already closed. We called the phone number listed and didn’t get an answer. Furthermore, the cups inside the cupboards were dirty as was most of the cabin. Certainly not what we had expected to have to have encountered for a family relaxing getaway. For more than $300 for a night, we definitely don’t feel it was worth it and wouldn’t recommend to family or friends.
My review is related not the caravan park. It is based on Victoria Daylesford Hepburn United Soccer Club. The goal could do with some sand packing.
Fantastic, will be back again and again.

Hello! I have been staying here in the park and I have to say the location is stunning. There is a lot of bird song and there are so many beautiful trees to look at.
The cabin I slept in was clean, the heater was warm and welcome. The bed was super comfortable with hotel grade linen.
Shower and bathroom were clean and excellent.
Staff in the office are super helpful and knowledgeable.
Managers were extremely friendly and helpful. Nice park with clean amenities.
Love, Love, Love this Park. Close to town. Great Park. Fantastic people. Great choices of cabins, caravan parking and rent sites.

We stayed in cabin 12. The receptionist allocated us a studio cabin but when we asked if it was pet friendly she changed our booking to a 6 berth cabin which cost us an additional $42.00. The first thing we noticed was a painting that had been hung sideways in the room. It appeared it was hung this way because of space limitations. The room was not clean there was mould on the curtains and on the window sills. There was laminate missing off cupboards and insufficient seating for 6 people. Had to wash our dishes before using them. Shampoo and conditioner provided for only 1 guest. The remainder 5 guests miss out. Paying $208.00 per night for this cabin is the most expensive we have paid ever and certainly not the best. Any to top things off the bed was well past it’s use by date and no wifi. I would not recommend this park to anyone

I fell in love with Daylesford from the moment I checked into the park. Despite freezing weather the access to Sequoia Lodge and its open fire and couches, meant we had a comfortable place to relax, watch tv and plan our adventures without being cooped up in the van. The site directly behind the lodge and adjacent amenities was ideal. I enjoyed being able to walk my dogs in the expansive grounds and over the road at the sports fields. Access to town was easy and the staff were extremely welcoming and helpful. We will be returning definitely.

Not a very good experience.Chose glamping and the service sucks.the bathroom n toilet were far apart from our staying place and at night it was too dark to even walk to the toilet. There was no breakfast provided as well. Went on a glamping before n each camp shld b provided with their own washroom n normally even breakfast is provided. It was not worth the price which was so high.

We were quite disappointed with this caravan park on our first visit to Daylesford.
The ammenities were not cleaned whilst we were there for 4 days. The scraps of toilet paper that were on the floor were never removed and the water in the mop bucket was filthy and never changed throughout that time.
The cottage shown on the website with the fireplace was freezing cold and not well maintained. The curtains were hanging off the rods and it wasn’t very presentable. The fire was set which was nice, but would be good if it was lit mid afternoon so it was warm when the guests arrived. With temperatures close to 0 it would have been appreciated.
The camp kitchen looked fine but way too cold to go and sit out in the evening without any heating or enclosed space.
Did a load of washing of towels and put in the dryer. A cycle cost $5. I spent $15 on the drying cycle and the towels were still wet and had to be hung in the van with the heater on overnight to get dry. I did report this and the office person gave me $5 to put through another cycle and asked me to let her know if it dried. It didn’t but nothing else was offered to rectify the issue.
Loved Daylesford but this park was a disappointment.

Staff were very helpful and friendly. Cabin was clean and roomy.
We stayed during a week in May. Very quite in the park and also in town.
Walking distance to the lake and town if you enjoy a good stroll.
Would not recommend the Farmers Arms Hotel. Use to be terrific but seems to have gone down hill. It is now very expensive and lost some of its charm.
Would be happy to revisit the Daylesford Holiday Park in the future.

Had a lovely.4 night stay at the Daylesford Caravan Park in a powered site with our 2 dogs. The staff were super friendly  and helpful and the showers were great and very clean. The only downside was a few things weren't working,  like the gas fire pit, 1of the 2 washing machines and 1 of the 2 driers available. Other than that we enjoyed a relaxing few days with plenty of space and close but hilly walks into town and Lake Daylesford.
I had a great stay at Dayelsford.  With a large Fifth Wheeler, I was given a great site.  They had a few problems with one of the amenity blocks but this was rectified very quickly.

We stayed in Elm 8 unit over the Anzac  weekend  April 2025 and paid $246.67 a night for 3 adults. Other members of our family were also staying there for the weekend as well. We arrived on the Thursday afternoon and when we arrived at our unit it was so disappointed with it . Cannot believe it has never been undated for about 40years not only that the place was filthy. We had taken our shoes off at the door and walked inside with my white socks on and by the time I put my case down in the bedroom they were black. The dishes and cutlery were dirty in the cupboards in the kitchenette. Spider webs everywhere. I continued to make the bunk bed up and went out to get something a few minutes later i returned to the room and there was the biggest spider in the bedroom on the floor. I’d hate to think of where that came from😡 The broom was full of hair and there was hair on the floor in the bathroom also in the shower 😡|All of our family members that stayed there were all disappointed with their accommodation as well their cabins were old , filthy and smelt like animals and. Needed updating to. |Our families gathered in the camp kitchen each night for dinner and that was not kept clean either.|The toilet blocks are also so very old and are in need of an update.|Think it’s about time they spent some money on the place for the prices they are charging | VERY DiSSAPPOINTED
For a caravan park which is a public space not to have toilet paper or soap in the men’s toilets is terrible. The toilets look like they have never been cleaned.for weeks.
Lovely park and very helpful staff

We enjoyed a lovely stay at Daylesford Holiday Park over Easter 2025. The park is picturesque and the fellow campers were respectful and quiet in the evening (we were in a caravan). The owners are lovely and there were great Easter offerings (paella van, coffee van, face painting, Easter Bunny visit). The amenities could do with an upgrade, but were clean. We would stay again.
Nice little caravan park, nestled in the surrounding of tall pines . Good walk into town with lots of cafes and shops.

A lovely park within walking distance of the main town. Fantastic markets on Saturday and Sunday.
Staff were really helpful and responsive. The park was a bit dry and dusty in some places but understandable because of the drought conditions. Two amenity buildings close to each other and a a well equipped camp kitchen provided us with all our needs.
We definitely recommend this park.
Fantastic location, with a short walk to the lake. The facilities are clean and well maintained. The management team are very friendly.
Wonderful hosts who were able to change our cabin to a very nice one due to issues with our first cabin, no trouble at all. Easy walk to the lake from the caravan park also. Will stay there again.

Worst park I’ve ever stayed at. Where do I start? Difficult to find as it’s in a sports ground. On arrival was given map to our site and it was like a treasure hunt. Map difficult and instructions from lady in office not accurate. Eventually found the site and couldn’t find the power or water connection. Back to the office where the lady said, oh didn’t I explain this part? Hook up is into another site which is not visible via extensions which then meander along the ground. Antiquated power board with no safety switch - very dangerous and not code. Neighbour informed us when she arrived power switch didn’t work on the neighbouring 3 sites. Massive old pine trees which is nice for shade but incredibly worrisome for falling limbs. On walking around the park found one cabin flattened by pine branch. Didn’t make us feel very safe. Our site was behind a retaining wall so we were literally stacked on top of each other. Our view was the underside of another van. Horrible. Now to the bathrooms where there is a mop bucket asking you to mop your own shower after use. Water runs hot without warning during showering and scolds you. Park filled with ancient permanent vans that are an eyesore
I don’t know what else to say…… oh there’s no dump point in the park, it’s outside near the sports ground.
And for all of this you pay a premium price. The 3 other parks we stayed at during our trip had swimming pools, grassed sites, and lovely facilities for less.
Unfortunately there isn’t much choice for caravan parks in Daylesford and the quality and care for this park absolutely reflects this. No need to care for the park as if you want to stay in Daylesford with your van, this is where you need to go.
Very relaxing and plenty to see and do. Take a favourite board game or good book to spend the evening before sleep.
Fantastic service & great staff
Great place to stay, we stayed 5 nights and thought it was a great park.
Fantastic park, great facilities.  Will happily come back again.
Nice campsites and lovely staff
Great park exceptionally clean bathroom and showers

Had an awesome stay here. The receptionist was super friendly and welcoming, which set the tone for the whole trip. The facilities are great—everything’s clean, well-kept, and there’s plenty of space. It’s a really chill spot in perfect location, just a short walk to the lake and walkable distance to the town. Highly recommend will definitely come back.
Called to book.... too expensive

Camped over Xmas and New Years and I just kept extending my stay! The site was so well maintained, staff were so polite, coffee van, pizza van and the pancakes were highlights! I’ll definitely be back as the location was fantastic too!

Lovely staff lovely place great location.
Highly reccomended 👌

My family and I stayed in a 2-bedroom cabin at Daylesford Holiday Park this week and had a wonderful experience. The accommodation was lovely—clean, comfortable, and well-equipped. While the cabin showed a little age, it didn’t detract from the overall charm and functionality, especially considering the excellent value for money.

The staff were incredibly friendly and
accommodating, ensuring our stay was smooth and enjoyable. The park's peaceful setting and proximity to local attractions made it the perfect getaway spot. I’d definitely recommend it to anyone looking for an affordable and relaxing holiday in Daylesford.
Nice place

What a park!
Generous size camping sites with lots of shade and privacy. Clean amenities and a very generous camp kitchen.
Located next to a sporting ground, so plenty of places for the dog to run around and stretch your legs after eating and drinking in town all day. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Good c/van park. Clean and tidy shower/toilet block.
Not sure why it needs that the guests need to use a code to get out and not just a sensor, but that is like that at few parks we've been at.
Level sites would be a great improvement, as it's always out with the leveling blocks to  get the van somewhere near level.
Still a good park and would happy return to do our frequent gold hunting around the GT. Thanks.

We stayed on a picturesque powered site with our van over cup weekend. Our site was very large with enough room for our friends and their swags. Amenities were clean and well maintained and the park is not too far from town. Aaron, Meagan and the staff were brilliant. So friendly and accommodating of all requests we made. They even put on breakfast for the entire campsite as a special treat on the Monday! Authentic, people who genuinely care about your experience. Highly recommend and we’ll be back!

We have stayed in this park before and it is a beautiful park we usually camp. This time we stayed in a cabin as our tent broke at the beginning of our road trip and we did appreciate that Daylesford could find a cabin for us. We stayed in the basic cabin which we had done throughout our trip through SA and VIC. The basic cabin at Daylesford was not nice. Basic or Standard usually means small but every cabin we had stayed in prior was modern and had everything that we needed and were comfortable. The ones at Daylesford were too old and dingy and the bathroom shower had no water pressure and the towel rack was only big enough for one towel and there was bo where to put our toothbrushes. The Cabin sleeps 4 but there is no way a family of 4 could stay there comfortably. We ended up walking to the shower block to use the shower as the one in our cabin was terrible. Also the bed was so uncomfortable the mattress was so firm. We paid just over $160 per night which was the second most expensive cabin we paid for on our 2 week trip and it was the only cabin I found uncomfortable and disappointing.

Have always, always been a Daylesford fan. Haven't been back since adopting a Bull Arab. Stayed at Daylesford Holiday Park. Thoroughly enjoyed my stay with Tiger.|Warm reception when signing in. Lovely lady at the front desk. Cabin had an ensuite which was a great addition for the price. Stayed 2 amazing nights and I am already planning our next trip. Highly recommended.
A great holiday park, however the shower cubicles are very small but overall extremely clean and well maintained.  The staff are very friendly and courteous.

Grand final weekend we stayed here, the park itself was nice, however we were on the non powered sites. Which had very limited flat ground, was quiet difficult to find a flat spot to set up 2 x camp trailers n 1 tent.
lucky it wasn't busy
The playground was a little dated but the kids enjoyed it

We booked a 3-day power site. The ground was very sloping, the toilet was far away, and we had to walk a long way to get there at night. The water for showering would suddenly become cold or hot. There were people making noises in the campsite until late for two consecutive nights, and no one cared. We decided to leave early

Pretty park,  made more appealing with the lovely individual garden touches. |Only downfall is the inadequate number of toilets and showers for a park this size,  with front block appearing to be closed( signage on door) and therefore all occupants using inadequately maintained block of 3 showers and 3 toilets.  Great location, close to town and lake.
Great park on edge of town centre. Facilities very clean and taken care of, staff were helpful.

Some sites are quite sloping however no problem for us. Price was high until the lady offered a discount. Would have preferred the lower rate straight up. Bit dicky getting through the gate when area unknown, I thought this was the entry to the footy ground. Perhaps better signage required. Staff friendly and helpful.

Very good location.
However:
No kids playroom
No swimming pool.
Internet its bad quality and cuts out after 2Gigs/day and its very strict in time limits

提供的地圖有夠複雜還表示的不清不楚
找到位置後還沒有提供該有的設備，連電也沒有提供，整個營地的設施配置還非常不方便，感受很差…

Nice place
Dated amenities
The entrance is very confusing as you have to turn into a sporting ground
We missed it and travelled up the road and it's VERY DIFFICULT trying to turn around with the van attached
Suggest better sinage at the gates to the sports ground ( there's some but not adequate when talking to other campers too)
Guy in office was niceties door handle at office os broken
Office was open and I turned the handle and it didn't work
Had to holler at ol mate sitting in the office through a closed window in after hrs room ...
Beautiful park, close to everything, very quiet in autumn. Had a great stay, will be back.

Cabin barely met basic needs from a hygiene and comfort perspective.
The weather was very cold. The unit had an ineffective split system and an ‘out of order’ gas space heater. The floors were extremely dirty. The couch needs replacing. Plumbing is ancient. Internet connection archaic and slow.
So substandard in many ways; there is no pride in presenting this as an accommodation offering in Daylesford.
Nice caravan park.  Very long layout.  Has clamping tents.  Take care entering from the main road.
Nice camping spot, dog friendly
Great campsite with clean amenities and friendly staff. Powered site was well maintained and just large enough to fit our tent and gazebo.
Very nice location to take a break.

My husband & myself booked an unpowered site at the daylesford caravan park from the 8th to the 11th March "24.We both came down with a gastric virus and decided to postpone our trip.I emailed the caravan park the day before our arrival to say that we would like to reschedule another time to come.The proprietor said they wouldnt allow a rescheduling of the booking

Daylesford/Hepburn Springs put on a wonderfully colour Pride Festival. The town was appropriately decorated in all the rainbow 🌈 colours and shimmering with Pride. The people I spoke with staying at the caravan park said there was a lot of talent about.

Thanks for hosting the Chillout festival!
It was so much fun and means a lot to the community.

We stayed on a powered site with our caravan. No grass. Site was all gravel with some paving stones.
The park has some magnificent shady trees and sites are easy to s access.
There are no sullage points and I noticed other campers not collecting their waste water, allowing it to spill onto the ground.
The big no no was that there is no dump point for toilet water. The nearest one is outside the park, across the road at the sporting oval.
They seriously need to install dump points outside the amenities blocks as most vans and RVs have toilets nowadays.
Staff are friendly and helpful.

I recently stayed here overnight in the unpowered camping area.
When I arrived I was greeted by friendly and helpful staff.
When I was about to leave the following day, my car wouldn't start and the staff were so wonderful and helpful to get my car going again.
I definitely recommend this park and the next time I stay in Daylesford will definitely be here.
Very relaxing
Great place to stay

Originally booked 1 night but liked it so much we stayed for a second.

Receptionist on entry was lovely and really helpful. Place is very clean and set amongst the trees. Kitchens and toilets have all the little extras you like.

They even have a TV room with a fire place all set up and ready to go. Nice and quiet to give you time to relax.
Put in a small powered site under Huge pine trees - dark, dirty, wet, car and caravan were filthy after just a few days

The Serenity Glamping Tent was visibly mouldy, smelly & severely run down. Dust, hair & dead bugs in the bed & kitchen draws. All the furniture was stained. Then attended the “teepee” for bathroom amenities to be horrified by the black mould on tent & floor. So unhygienic & disgusting. We could NOT stay here and left within an hour. How can you justify charging $190 per night?!

Can't comment on rooms We had van site. Customer service was brilliant. Sites was spacious , Amenities were clean and well maintained. Sites are a bit hilly. But comes with package and area you are in. Will return
Plenty of room for camping or bring your own caravan or campervan. A must visit.

Beautiful location, truly dog friendly and wonderful management. Only our second trip in our caravan and first time with the dogs. Powered site which was generous in size. Clean facilities and so peaceful. Thank you for a wonderful stay we will be back!!
Staff excellent a big help with camping amenities kept clean stayed with van and the had a reasonable cost for storage of van

Just spent our second Grand Final weekend here in this beautiful park. It’s an easy walk to town, lovely ammenities, such friendly staff. We would highly recommend it!
Our group of 5 vans are all coming back again next year!
Thank you so much
Felicity Carson

Thanks Daylesford Holiday Park for a really lovely and peaceful "first weekend of Spring" 3 day getaway. We've stayed in the "Barkroyal" cabin before, but this time we thought we'd bring our camper up and enjoy one of your powered sites. Easy booking process, easy parking and set up, and we were really pleased with our site next to the camp kitchen and right in-between a choice of bathroom amenities. I especially love how the camp kitchen has open walls because it creates a friendly atmosphere. The park is so well maintained and you can really get a sense of how much pride is put into the place. I also adored all the little pops of colourful daffodils here and there! We also enjoyed walking down to the Lake and around the sporting complex and ovals. So thank you for a pleasant stay, and we'll see you again soon! Ron & Rachel (& Billie the dog hahaha)

We booked the glamping  accommodations in holiday park and it was such a pleasant and unique experience for us. A perfect place for weekend getaway and cozy stays !

A perfect holiday park for all age groups !
Have many options for accommodations like camping site and cabins !

It’s really important to review the facts. We stayed in a caravan site, difficult to get level in the hilly terrain. There is no sullage outlet. Yuk. Amenities block - owner has known for a long time that the showers are leaking, nothing is done. Camp kitchen- please enclose. Please provide normal camp kitchen crockery, etc.
so much potential here, just need a great innovator
